idea-plugin.name: Copy All Problems
idea-plugin.description:
Adds an action to copy every diagnostic (errors, warnings, weak warnings, info) for the currently active file to the clipboard, one per line, formatted as:

src/app/main.py:42:5 [WARNING] Unresolved reference 'foo'

Useful when you need to paste a file's full problem list into an external tool (chat, ticket, code review) and the standard Problems window copy doesn't include line numbers in your build.

How to use: right-click in the editor → Copy All Problems with Line Numbers, or press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+P (Windows/Linux) / ⌘+Shift+Alt+P (Mac).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.12
  • Problem locations are now reported as a project-relative path (e.g. src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:42:5) instead of just the file name, so files that share a name across different directories are no longer ambiguous. Files outside the project fall back to the plain file name.
  • Broadened IDE compatibility: the plugin now installs on IntelliJ-platform IDEs back to 2022.2 (build 222), not just 2024.2+. It is built to Java 17 bytecode, so a single build runs across 2022.2 through 2026.1.
  • Updated build-time dependencies to patched versions to clear security advisories. No change to the shipped plugin.
1.0.11
  • New Notification style setting (Settings → Tools → Copy All Problems): choose how the result is reported — modal popup with OK (default, unchanged from 1.0.1+), balloon notification, editor hint near the caret, or silent (no notification at all). Pick a non-modal style to skip the OK click.
1.0.10
  • Replaced internal DaemonCodeAnalyzerImpl.getHighlights(...) call with the recommended DaemonCodeAnalyzerEx.processHighlights(...) entry point, clearing the JetBrains Marketplace "internal API usage" warning.
  • Added LICENSE.md (MIT) and a settings-panel screenshot to the README.
1.0.9
  • Filtered out non-problem highlights that the Problems tool window already hides: symbol-coloring annotations (severity below INFORMATION, e.g. "Automatically declared based on the expected type") and annotator-only INFORMATION markers (URL hyperlinks like "Open in browser (Ctrl+Click, Ctrl+B)", identifier highlighting, etc.).
  • Real INFORMATION-level inspection results (e.g. unused-symbol hints) are still included when the INFORMATION checkbox is on.
1.0.8
  • Internal build maintenance: fixed build.gradle.kts syntax and bumped the Gradle wrapper to 9.x for compatibility with IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin 2.16.0.
1.0.7
  • Added action icon shown in the editor right-click menu and Tools menu (light + dark variants).
1.0.6
  • Restored full release history in the plugin metadata (1.0.0 through 1.0.5 were missing from the change-notes panel).
1.0.5
  • Added plugin icon (light + dark variants).
1.0.4
  • Added STYLE_SUGGESTION severity checkbox.
  • Added "Include other / unknown severities" catch-all toggle for forward compatibility.
1.0.3
  • New Settings → Tools → Copy All Problems panel.
  • UI checkboxes for ERROR / WARNING / WEAK WARNING / INFORMATION / GRAMMAR_ERROR / TYPO / SERVER_PROBLEM severities.
  • Output format options: include column number, include [SEVERITY] tag, sort by severity first.
  • GRAMMAR_ERROR unchecked by default to reduce noise.
1.0.2
  • Hardcoded GRAMMAR_ERROR filter (later replaced by the settings panel in 1.0.3).
1.0.1
  • Switched from balloon notifications to modal popup for compatibility with PyCharm 2025.x (build 251+).
  • Bumped target IntelliJ Platform to 2025.1.
  • Bumped Kotlin compiler to 2.1.0.
1.0.0
  • Initial release.
  • Action: Copy All Problems with Line Numbers (Editor right-click, Tools menu, ⌘+Shift+Alt+P).
  • Output format: filename:line:col [SEVERITY] description, one per line.

action.text: Copy All Problems with Line Numbers
action.description: Copies all diagnostics for the current file to clipboard
